---
title: "Cognitive Compilation and Semantic IR"
chapter_id: "cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir"
part_id: "planning-memory-reasoning-execution"
status: "conceptual"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "cognitive_compilation"
  - "planforge_compiler_arch"
  - "genesiscode"
  - "treellm"
  - "viea"
evidence_level: "argument"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Assigned source texts have not yet been ingested or summarized."
  - "No Codex tests have been implemented or run."
  - "Chapter prose is a scaffold, not a completed manuscript draft."
---

# Cognitive Compilation and Semantic IR

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir` |
| Part | Part II - Planning, Memory, Reasoning, and Execution |
| Status | conceptual |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `cognitive_compilation`, `planforge_compiler_arch`, `genesiscode`, `treellm`, `viea` |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source ingestion state | Source records assigned; source texts not yet ingested. |
| Test state | Planned only; no tests have been run. |

## Drafting guardrail

This stub is derived from `book_structure.json` and the source inventory only. It does not claim that the listed source documents have been ingested, summarized, or independently verified.

## Problem

LLM workflows need intermediate representations that separate requirements, plans, artifacts, repairs, and target runtimes.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

Generate-from-prompt workflows entangle requirements and implementation, drift over long contexts, and make incremental repair fragile.

## Core claim

[cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.core, support: argument] Cognitive compilation should transform goals into semantic intermediate representations that can be optimized, verified, repaired, and targeted.

## Mechanism

- Represent requirements, semantics, dependencies, artifacts, and target constraints explicitly.
- Perform incremental repair over IR rather than re-prompting from scratch.
- Compile IR into jobs, code, schemas, or documents.

## Interfaces

- Planning produces or consumes IR.
- Execution receives target-specific artifacts.
- Verification checks IR-to-output preservation.

## Invariants

- IR preserves declared requirements.
- Repair changes are localized and auditable.
- Target compilation records assumptions.

## Failure modes

- IR drift.
- Compiler hallucination.
- Repair that breaks earlier requirements.

## Minimal implementation

A semantic IR sketch and compile/verify/repair loop for one artifact type.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Requirement preservation test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Incremental repair regression test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Target compilation audit | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Planned use |
|---|---|---|
| `cognitive_compilation` | Cognitive Compilation | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Compiler framing for LLM-centered planning, semantic IR, target compilation, incremental repair. |
| `planforge_compiler_arch` | PlanForge: A Compiler Architecture for AI Task Orchestration | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Later/alternate PlanForge framing. Prefer highest-quality/latest content after comparison. |
| `genesiscode` | GenesisCode | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Tiny pure calculus + obligations + provenance for auditable AI-symbiotic programming. |
| `treellm` | TreeLLM | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Hierarchical semantic token system for grounded, efficient, explainable language modeling. |
| `viea` | Verified Intent-to-Execution Architecture | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Keystone source. Human intent -> command contracts -> artifacts -> routing -> runtime targets -> verification -> deployment -> feedback. |

## Summary

This chapter currently establishes the structural slot for this layer of the ASI stack. The next drafting pass should ingest assigned sources, separate source-derived claims from design hypotheses, and update Appendix C when support states change.
